text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Priya Nair 
Consultant Neurologist  
Centre for Epilepsy  
King’s College Hospital, Denmark Hill, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Priya Nair, 

Martha Ellison, DOB: 22-08-1942, Hospital No: K947210 NHS No. 8015623391  
Flat 2B, Rosewood Care Home, 14 Brook Lane, London, SE15 4JP  

Diagnosis: 

Seizure disorder in a bedbound older adult; seizure semiology remains unclear; antiseizure medication tolerance issues reported 

Aetiology: 

Unclear; no prior neuroimaging available in records; historic falls and possible hypoxic episode two years ago as per caregiver report 

Other Medical Problems: 

Hypertension; type 2 diabetes managed with oral agents; chronic lower limb oedema; pressure-area care with alternating air mattress; mild cognitive impairment suspected but not formally assessed 

Management Plan: 

- Continue current carbamazepine 200 mg twice daily for now; no dose change today pending review of levels and interactions.  
- Arrange community phlebotomy for carbamazepine level, FBC, U&Es, LFTs, and HbA1c.  
- Request domiciliary EEG via community neurophysiology service if feasible; if not, consider ambulatory recording.  
- Caregiver to maintain a seizure diary, noting timing, duration, description, potential triggers, and recovery profile.  
- Initiate bone health review via GP (vitamin D level, calcium, consideration of supplementation) given immobility and antiseizure therapy.  
- Safety measures: ensure side-lying recovery position after events, padded cot-sides as already in place, and continue regular pressure-area repositioning.  
- Review in four months via domiciliary visit or video if preferred by caregiver; earlier review if events cluster or there is injury, prolonged recovery, or suspected status. 

Summary of Current Status: 

I assessed Martha Ellison at home with the primary nurse caregiver present, as the patient is bedbound and largely reliant on carers for personal care and transfers. According to the nurse’s log and direct report, there have been about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months. These events are described as brief episodes of unresponsiveness with staring, sometimes accompanied by right hand tremor and lip-smacking, lasting under two minutes, followed by 10–20 minutes of confusion and fatigue. No clear precipitant identified; however, two episodes occurred in the evening after missed meals during intercurrent urinary symptoms. There has been no tongue-biting or witnessed head injury. No persistent focal neurological deficit is reported post-event. 

Medication Tolerability and Adherence: 

Carbamazepine 200 mg twice daily is administered by carers with good adherence documented on the MAR chart. The nurse notes intermittent dizziness late morning, which could reflect peak effect or orthostatic change; blood pressures will be checked sitting and supine by the community nursing team. No rash has been observed. Sodium levels have not been checked in the last year; hence the arranged blood tests. 

Investigations and Information Gaps: 

No prior brain imaging is available within our shared records. Given mobility limitations, I have requested domiciliary EEG and will consider brain imaging if logistics permit and if results would meaningfully change management. We have also requested a copy of the full medication list from the GP to review for potential interactions (notably with antihypertensives and diabetes medication). 

Care and Support: 

The caregiver has an effective seizure observation routine and will continue to document events. They also requested guidance on when to call emergency services; I advised calling 999 if any seizure lasts longer than five minutes, if there are repeated seizures without full recovery, significant injury, or new breathing difficulties. 

Please feel free to contact me if there are any concerns or if there is a sudden change in the frequency or nature of events. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: there have been about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months
about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: 'there have been about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months.' This provides a clear numeric range and time frame. Normalize to the allowed format as a range per month period: '2 to 3 per 3 month'. No other frequencies are given, and there is no indication of seizure freedom for 6 months.
seizure_frequency_number: there have been about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months
reference: about 2 to 3 seizures in the last three months
